body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## đźŽŻ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A bot-filed documentation issue for `Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis` requesting fixes to the README (wrong package name in install command), enriched Key Concepts section, and inline code snippets â€” triggered following the `1.1.2` â†’ `1.2.0-beta.1` version bump.

<details>
<summary>đź“‹ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis` (version bump to `1.2.0-beta.1`)
**Affected API:** README documentation, `tests/Samples/` directory
**Scenarios:**
- Wrong package name (`Azure.ResourceManager.AnalysisServices`) in `dotnet add package` install command
- No inline code examples in the `Examples` section (only external link)
- Key Concepts section lacks descriptions of `AnalysisServerResource`, `AnalysisServerCollection`, `AnalysisServerData`
- No `tests/Samples/` directory for snippet injection via `Update-Snippets.ps1`

**Root ask:** Fix the README and add a `Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` test file with snippet-backed code examples for create, get, list, and delete operations.
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź”Ž Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue filed by the `azure-sdk` bot to address accumulated doc debt. No runtime behavior is affected.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Verify the README install command uses `Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis` (not `Azure.ResourceManager.AnalysisServices`)
2. Review the proposed `Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` file in the issue body for accuracy against actual SDK types (`AnalysisResourceSku`, `AnalysisSkuTier`, etc.)
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 analysisservices` after creating the sample file to inject snippets into README placeholders
4. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm no regressions
</details>

<details>
<summary>đźŹ·ď¸Ź Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` â€” package is `Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis`, which falls under the management SDK family
- **Service:** `Analysis Services` â€” service directory is `sdk/analysisservices/`; label confirmed to exist (color `#e99695`)
- **Confidence:** High â€” package name and service directory are explicitly stated in the issue body; no ambiguity between services or categories
- **Author:** `github-actions[bot]` â€” matched bot allowlist; `bot` label added
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź‘Ą Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 903) â€” issue has `Mgmt` label, which satisfies the single-label requirement; first match when scanning bottom-to-top
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @ArthurMa1978
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@ArthurMa1978`; "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** All `%<service> %Mgmt` entries (lines 915â€“1221) require a specific second label (e.g., "ARM", "Compute - Fleet") not present in this issue's labels, so none matched. `%Mgmt` catch-all at line 903 was the first match. `%Analysis Services` entry at line 542 was encountered after the catch-all in the bottom-to-top scan and was not reached.
</details>
